channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: The Punisher: One Last Kill – Frank Castle’s Struggle Between Revenge and Redemption
item.description: The one-off TV special 'The Punisher: One Last Kill' is now available on Disney+, featuring Jon Bernthal reprising his role as Frank Castle. Directed by Reinaldo Marcus Green and co-written by Berntha...

item.title: Russia’s Sarmat ICBM: Capabilities, Deployment, and Strategic Significance
item.description: On 12 May 2026, Russia successfully launched its newest heavy liquid-fuel intercontinental ballistic missile (ICBM), the Sarmat, marking a significant milestone in its strategic weapons programme. The...

item.title: Congress Selects VD Satheesan as Kerala Chief Minister After IUML Support and Grassroots Backing
item.description: After extensive internal deliberations and negotiations, the Congress party has officially announced VD Satheesan as the new Chief Minister of Kerala. The decision was declared on May 14, 2026, by AIC...

item.title: IMF Approves USD 375.5 Million for Tanzania, GDP Expected to Grow 5.9% in 2026
item.description: In early 2026, Tanzania's economic situation shows a mix of steady growth and manageable inflation. According to the National Bureau of Statistics, headline inflation slightly decreased from 3.3% in J...

item.title: Presidential Commission Recommends Major Tax Reforms to Improve Tanzania's Business Environment
item.description: On March 18, 2026, the Presidential Commission on Tax Reforms, led by Ambassador Ombeni Sefue, handed over a detailed report with 284 proposals to President Samia Suluhu Hassan aimed at fixing Tanzani...

item.title: Congress Selects VD Satheesan as Kerala Chief Minister over KC Venugopal
item.description: The Congress party in Kerala has chosen VD Satheesan as the new Chief Minister, bypassing senior leader KC Venugopal. This decision comes amid internal deliberations within the party, with several fac...

item.title: Government Initiates Investigation Over Alleged Cambridge Exam Paper Leaks
item.description: The Interior Ministry has directed the National Cyber Crime Investigation Agency (NCCIA) to look into the alleged leakage of Cambridge examination papers in Pakistan. This decision came after confirma...

item.title: Arlene Dahl: A Hollywood Icon of Beauty and Business
item.description: Arlene Dahl, born in 1925, was a prominent actress during Hollywood's Golden Age, recognized for her striking red hair and elegant presence. She rose to fame as a leading lady at MGM in the 1950s, app...

item.title: Corient Expands Family Office Services to Canada, Targeting Ultra-High-Net-Worth Clients
item.description: Corient Private Wealth LLC, a Miami-based wealth management firm, is entering the Canadian market to offer family office services to ultra-high-net-worth (UHNW) clients. The expansion, planned for Jun...

item.title: UK GDP grows 0.3% in March, beating forecasts despite early Iran conflict effects
item.description: The UK economy expanded by 0.3% in March, surpassing analysts' expectations of a slight contraction, according to official figures from the Office for National Statistics (ONS). This growth occurred d...

item.title: UK airports to allow children aged eight and nine to use e-gates
item.description: From 8 July, children aged eight and nine returning to the UK from abroad will be permitted to use e-gates at airports and other re-entry points, according to the Home Office. To use the e-gates, chil...

item.title: Reflections on the Significance of Thursdays in Personal and National History
item.description: For Huzaifa Jega, Thursdays carry a special kind of magic. From personal milestones like the first day of school, the birth of siblings, meeting his wife, and earning his first million naira, to natio...

item.title: Wes Streeting: From East End Childhood to Leading Labour Ambitions
item.description: Wes Streeting, the Health Secretary and MP for Ilford North, has emerged as a potential successor to Keir Starmer as Labour leader, despite being considered controversial within his own party. Born in...

item.title: High Court Orders Safaricom to Compensate Subscribers After Data Breach
item.description: In a landmark ruling, the Nairobi High Court has directed Safaricom PLC to compensate 11 subscribers Sh900,000 each, amounting to Sh9.9 million, for a massive breach of personal data that occurred bet...

item.title: Supreme Court clarifies requirements for forensic drug test reports
item.description: ISLAMABAD – The Supreme Court of Pakistan has finally resolved a long-standing dispute regarding forensic drug test reports. A five-judge bench, led by Justice Jamal Khan Mandokhail, declared that gov...

item.title: Allan Thigo Cremated According to His Wishes, Family and Friends Pay Tribute
item.description: Former Harambee Stars and Gor Mahia midfielder Allan Thigo, aged 77, was cremated at the Hindu Crematorium in Kisumu, fulfilling his last wish. Thigo, who passed away on Saturday after a battle with p...

item.title: Song Weilong Named Ambassador for Prestigious Luxury Brands
item.description: Song Weilong, the popular actor known for his role in the Chinese drama 'Shine On Me,' has been officially named the new ambassador for modern luxury brands. This milestone marks a significant step in...

item.title: Inquiry Launched After Insect Found in RML Doctors’ Hostel Mess Food
item.description: At Dr Ram Manohar Lohia Hospital, a complaint has triggered an inquiry after a cockroach was reportedly found in dal served at the doctors’ hostel mess on Tuesday. A hostel resident shared photographs...

item.title: Melbourne Symphony confirms donor funds won’t be used in court case with pianist
item.description: The Melbourne Symphony Orchestra (MSO) has moved to reassure its supporters that philanthropic donations will not be used to cover legal expenses as it prepares to defend itself in a significant Fair ...

item.title: North West floods highlight need for improved disaster preparedness and infrastructure
item.description: Heavy floods across the North West province have highlighted serious shortcomings in the region's disaster preparedness and emergency response systems. According to Prof Christo Coetzee from the Afric...
